status.constitutionalAuthorityStatementText:
[Congressional Record Volume 171, Number 1 (Friday, January 3, 2025)]
[House]
From the Congressional Record Online through the Government Publishing Office [www.gpo.gov]
By Mr. BEGICH:
H.R. 42.
Congress has the power to enact this legislation pursuant
to the following:
Article 1, Section 8
The single subject of this legislation is:
Excluding all amounts distributed or benefits provided by a
Settlement Trust to blind, disabled, and Alaska Native Elders
and their descendants when determining eligibility for
governmental benefits.
[Page H37]
status.title: Alaska Native Settlement Trust Eligibility Act

summaries.text:
Alaska Native Settlement Trust Eligibility Act

This bill excludes certain settlement trust payments to an Alaska Native or descendant of an Alaska Native who is aged, blind, or disabled for purposes of determining the individual's eligibility for certain need-based federal programs (e.g., the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program).

Alaska Native Settlement Trust Eligibility Act

This act excludes certain settlement trust payments to an Alaska Native or descendant of an Alaska Native who is aged, blind, or disabled for purposes of determining the individual's eligibility for certain need-based federal programs (e.g., the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program).
